q3_fy26_gross_margin_actual_pct.src: Micron 8-K / investor press release, non-GAAP, reported 2026-06-24

sca_total_value_b.src: web search — 16 Strategic Customer Agreements, 5-yr term 2026-2030, ~20% DRAM/~33% NAND volume, $22B customer commitments incl ~$18B cash; multiple outlets (Tom's Hardware, Yahoo Finance, Investing.com) citing Q3 FY26 earnings call slides

q4_annualized_eps_estimate.src: judgment — Q4 guide midpoint EPS $31.00 x 4, a simple annualized proxy, not a full-year build

blended_ntm_eps_working_estimate.src: judgment — midpoint blend of implied_ntm_eps_backed_out and q4_annualized_eps_estimate, held loosely

multiple_bear.src: judgment — trough-ish multiple applied to a lower, non-SCA-covered EPS assumption (~$85)

multiple_base.src: judgment — modest re-rate from today's compressed 6.4x as SCA thesis partially validates

multiple_bull.src: judgment — re-rate toward 'AI-infrastructure supplier' multiple, roughly matching analyst consensus

multiple_peer_median.src: not pulled this touch — MU's own cyclical-memory ruler doesn't use a peer-multiple anchor the way TSM's does; consider adding SK Hynix / Samsung memory-segment multiples at a future touch

analyst_consensus_target_high.src: web search — cited 'five-star analyst' target per public.com forecast page, Jul 2026

notes: This update was triggered ~17 days after Q3 FY26 earnings (June 24) had already reported and the thesis had gone stale (AS_OF_DATE was still 2026-06-03, pre-print). Q3 actuals blew past the OLD bull case on every metric (revenue, margin, Q4 guide). The single most important new fact is the $100B in 16 Strategic Customer Agreements — 5-year, binding, take-or-pay contracts running 2026-2030 with floor margins 'well beyond historical peaks' — which is direct evidence AGAINST the pull-forward interpretation of AI memory demand (management is signing 5-year commitments, not selling into a spot panic). Despite the blowout print, the stock is actually BELOW its pre-earnings level (from $1,059 to $979, roughly -8% net over 3 weeks) — a 'sell the news' / persistent cyclical-skepticism pattern worth tracking. New competitive risk surfaced this quarter: Samsung passed NVIDIA/AMD HBM4 certification and started mass production FIRST, ahead of Micron — the old bull case's 'HBM4 share gains vs SK Hynix' framing needs to now also account for Samsung. multiple_peer_median intentionally left null this touch (see inputs) rather than fabricated.
